title: DAY 06 — CRM-M6 — NDR (NON-DELIVERY REPORT) MANAGEMENT — DAY 1 OF 2

questions.question: What does NDR stand for, and what triggers it?

options.text: Non-Dispatch Report — raised by the warehouse when an order is not packed within the SLA window
Non-Delivery Report — raised by the courier when a delivery attempt fails
New Dispatch Request — raised by CRM when a COD order needs re-confirmation before shipping
Non-Delivery Receipt — raised by the customer when they have not received their order within 7 days

questions.question: What is the consequence if an NDR is not actioned within 24 hours?

options.text: The shipment is placed on hold and the customer is automatically refunded
The NDR is escalated to the CRM Asst Manager for manual review
The shipment is marked RTO (Return to Origin) — the order is returned to the warehouse and the customer does not receive their watch
The courier partner charges an additional re-attempt fee to Sylvi

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the four documented NDR failure reasons?

options.text: Customer not available
Wrong address
Payment failure at delivery
Refused delivery

questions.question: At the start of the day, before opening Shiprocket, what must the CRM Executive do first?

options.text: Send the day-start Shopify screenshot to the `customer-care` Slack channel
Log in to FIMS and check the Follow Up tab for pending orders
Check the NDR sheet for any open items from the previous day — these are the highest priority
Open BIK and clear all pending chats from the previous day before actioniing NDRs

questions.question: What is the correct sequence of steps to reach the NDR action panel in Shiprocket?

options.text: Shiprocket → Orders → Failed Deliveries → Select NDR → Act
Shiprocket → Delivery Boost → Unsuccessful Deliveries → Filter (Input Required = Yes) → View Details → Act on Shipment
Shiprocket → Reports → NDR Summary → Open NDR → Action Required → Proceed
Shiprocket → Dashboard → Active Shipments → Filter NDR → View → Action

questions.question: Why must the filter "Select Input Required = Yes" be applied in the Unsuccessful Deliveries panel?

options.text: To see only NDRs assigned to the current agent — other agents' NDRs are filtered out
To display only NDRs that have already been resolved — for daily reporting purposes
To show only NDRs that require CRM action — filtering out those already actioned or not yet requiring input
The filter has no functional effect — it is applied for UI display purposes only

questions.question: When using Path A (Call Buyer) in Shiprocket, which phone number must be selected for the outbound call?

options.text: The agent's personal mobile number — for faster customer identification
The customer's registered mobile number from FIMS
Company's Contact — never use a personal number
The CRM Manager's number — all outbound NDR calls must originate from the manager's line

questions.question: During a Path A call, the customer provides a new mobile number they want the courier to use. What must the Executive do in Shiprocket?

options.text: Note the new number in the NDR sheet Comments column only — Shiprocket does not support number updates
Update the Alternate Mobile Number field in Shiprocket before initiating the call action
Cancel this NDR and create a new one with the updated number
Share the new number with the courier directly via the `after-sale-services` Slack channel

questions.question: What is mandatory after every Path A (Call Buyer) action in Shiprocket, before clicking "Update Details"?

options.text: A screenshot of the FIMS order status taken at the time of the call
Uploading Proof of Communication — a screenshot or recording of the conversation with the buyer
Sending a WhatsApp follow-up message to the customer confirming the call was made
Notifying the CRM Asst Manager via Slack that a call was made on the NDR

questions.question: A customer is called on an NDR and confirms they want to proceed — they will be home for the next delivery attempt. What is the correct action in Shiprocket?

options.text: Path C — Mark RTO, as the customer has not received the package yet
Path B — Update Delivery Details with the customer's re-confirmation
Select re-attempt in the failed delivery options and add the re-attempt date; remark: "Customer confirmed — re-attempt on [date]"
Close the NDR as resolved — no further action needed once the customer confirms

questions.question: A customer called on an NDR says the address on the shipment has the wrong pincode. They provide the correct pincode. Which action path is used?

options.text: Path A — Call Buyer again to re-confirm the full corrected address verbally
Path C — Mark RTO, as the original order had an address error
Path D — Report a Shipment Issue, as address errors are a courier problem
Path B — Update Delivery Details with the corrected address, with a remark explaining the change

questions.question: A customer tells the CRM Executive on an NDR call that they no longer want the order and want it cancelled. Which action path applies?

options.text: Path A — note the customer's request as a remark and initiate a re-attempt anyway
Path B — update the delivery address to a different location per the customer's request
Path C — Mark RTO with reason: "Customer refused delivery — RTO initiated"
Path D — report this as a shipment issue and request a re-delivery attempt from the courier

questions.question: Which path is used when a fake delivery attempt is suspected?

options.text: Path A — Call Buyer and ask the customer to wait for the next re-attempt
Path B — Update Delivery Details with the correct address to ensure the courier finds it next time
Path D — Report a Shipment Issue, selecting "Fake Attempt" as the issue type, with Proof of Buyer Intent uploaded
Path C — Mark RTO, as fake attempts indicate the courier will not successfully deliver

meta.criteria: Fake Attempt Detection & Path D Execution

questions.question: Which of the following is a sign that a delivery attempt may have been fake?

options.text: The customer placed a COD order and the NDR reason is "Wrong address"
The customer confirms they were home, the courier never called, and the address is correct and traceable
The courier attempted delivery at the correct address but the customer was not home
The NDR was raised on the same day the order was dispatched

meta.criteria: Fake Attempt Detection & Path D Execution

questions.question: After using Path D to report a fake attempt, where must the escalation also be logged (in addition to the NDR sheet)?

options.text: FIMS → OMS → Shopify → Order Comments section
The `customer-care` Slack channel, with the AWB number and fake attempt details
Style Feathers Web Tracker → NDR tab — with agent name, status, escalation via, and issue type
Daily Work — CRM-2026.xlsx → IBC Calls tab — as a separate complaint entry

meta.criteria: Fake Attempt Detection & Path D Execution

questions.question: In the NDR sheet, what does the "BPR" column record?

options.text: Whether the buyer's payment was received (Bank Payment Record)
Whether Proof of Communication with the buyer was uploaded during this NDR attempt (Buyer Proof of communication — Yes/No)
The buyer's preferred re-delivery time window (Buyer Preference for Re-delivery)
Whether the buyer was physically present during the delivery attempt (Buyer Presence Record)

questions.question: An order has received two failed NDR attempts. The Executive is logging the second attempt in the NDR sheet. Which columns belong specifically to this second attempt?

options.text: Date NDR 2, NDR 2 Reason, Note NDR 2, Fake Attempt 2, BPR NDR 2, NDR 2 By
Date NDR 2, NDR 2 Reason, Status, RTO Reason
NDR 2 Reason, Note NDR 2, All BPR, Escalated Date
Date NDR 2, NDR 2 Reason, Note NDR 2, Tracking Status Update Date

questions.question: What condition must be true for the "All BPR" column in the NDR sheet to show "Yes"?

options.text: At least one of the three BPR fields is Yes
The order has been delivered successfully after the NDR was resolved
All three BPR fields (BPR NDR 1, BPR NDR 2, BPR NDR 3) must be Yes — it is auto-calculated
The Asst Manager has reviewed and approved all three NDR attempts

questions.question: After an RTO is confirmed, what is the difference in customer notification between a COD order and a Prepaid order?

options.text: COD orders require a full refund via bank transfer; Prepaid orders require the customer to place a new order
Both require the same communication — delivery failed and the order is being returned
COD orders require no further action (no payment was collected); Prepaid orders require a refund to be initiated as per the Return & Replacement SOP
Prepaid orders are auto-refunded by Shiprocket; COD orders must be manually marked as closed

meta.criteria: RTO Conditions, Customer Notification & Common Mistakes

questions.question: Which of the following is a documented common mistake in NDR handling?

options.text: Uploading a screenshot of the customer's WhatsApp reply as proof of communication
Calling the customer using the Company's Contact option in Shiprocket
Initiating RTO without calling the customer first — always attempt a call before marking RTO
Filling the Fake Attempt column as Yes when the customer reports a fake delivery

meta.criteria: RTO Conditions, Customer Notification & Common Mistakes
